questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
An electron is orbiting in a circular orbit of radius r under the influence of a constant strong magnetic field of strength B. Assuming that Bohr's postulate regarding the quantisation of angular momentum holds good for this electron, find
(a) the allowed values of the radius ' $r$ ' of the orbit.
(Assume that the charge on the electron is -e and the mass of the electron is m.)
questions.solution: For the electron, we can write, $\mathrm{m} \frac{\mathrm{dW}}{\mathrm{dQ}} \%=\frac{1}{4} \times 100=25 \%=\mathrm{evB}$ or, $\frac{\mathrm{mv}_{\max }}{(\mathrm{T} / 4)}=\frac{2 \mathrm{mg}}{\pi}=\int_{\mathrm{R}}^{2 R}\left(p_0+\frac{4 S}{r}\right) 4 \pi \mathrm{r}^2 d r$...(i) Using the Bohr quantisation condition, $\mathrm{mvr}=\frac{3 \mathrm{R}^2}{2}=\mathrm{n} \frac{\mathrm{~m}}{2 \mathrm{f}} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{~T}}{9 \mu}},$ $\mathrm{n} \rightarrow$ integer. or, $v r=\frac{n}{2 f} \sqrt{\frac{T}{4 \mu}}$...(ii) (a) $\mathrm{r}^2=\frac{\mathrm{K}_2}{\mathrm{~K}_1-\mathrm{K}_2}=\frac{2}{1}$ or, $\mathrm{r}=\frac{\mathrm{K}_1}{\mathrm{~K}_2}=\frac{3}{2} \frac{\sigma^2}{2 \varepsilon_0}=\frac{\mathrm{P}_1}{8}+\frac{\sigma^2}{2 \varepsilon_0}=\mathrm{P}_0+\frac{4 \mathrm{~T}}{2 \mathrm{r}} \mathrm{a}_0$...(iii)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows a circular region of radius $R=\sqrt{3} m$ which has a uniform magnetic field $B=0.2 T$ directed into the plane of the figure. A particle having mass $m=\mathbf{2 g}$, speed $v=0.3 \mathbf{m} / \mathrm{s}$ and charge $q=1 \mathbf{m C}$ is projected along the radius of the circular region as shown in figure. Calculate the angular deviation produced in the path of the particle as it comes out of the magnetic field. Neglect any other force apart from the magnetic force. (mark the answer in degree)
questions.solution:
Radius of circular path of the particle is $r=\frac{m v}{q B}=\frac{\mathbf{2} \times \mathbf{1 0}^{-3} \times \mathbf{0 . 3}}{\mathbf{1 0}^{-3} \times \mathbf{0 . 5}}=\mathbf{3} \mathrm{m}$ C is centre of the circular path. Particle enters the field at $A$ and leaves at $B$.
$\begin{aligned} & \tan \theta=\frac{R}{r}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & \theta=30^{\circ} \end{aligned}$ Deviation $=2 \theta=60^{\circ}$ [A careful observation tells that direction of velocity at $B$ is along the radius of the given circle ].
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A non conducting non-magnetic rod having square crosssection of side $L / 4$ is suspended from a rigid support as shown in the figure. A light and small coil of 600 turns is wrapped tightly at the left end of the rod where uniform magnetic field B exists in vertically downward direction. Air of density $\rho$ hits the half of the right part of the rod with velocity v as shown in the figure. What should be the current (in mA ) in the coil so that rod remains horizontal? [Assume air particles come to rest after colliding with the rod] [Take : $\rho=1.05 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3, \mathrm{v}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, $\mathrm{B}=1 \mathrm{~T}, \mathrm{~L}=2 \mathrm{~m}]$
questions.solution:
For the rod to remain horizontal, $\tau_{\text {net }}=0$
$\tau_{\text {air }}=\rho\left(\frac{L}{4} \times \frac{L}{2}\right) \cdot v \cdot v \frac{3 L}{4} ; \tau_{\text {mag }}=\vec{\mu} \times \vec{B}=N i\left(\frac{L}{4}\right)^2 B \Rightarrow I=\frac{\tau_{\text {air }}}{N B\left(\frac{L}{4}\right)^2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A wire of length $L$ is wound on a spherical form, and a current $I$ is passed through it. The coil is then placed in a uniform magnetic field of induction B. Find the number of turns so that the torque on it to be maximum.
questions.solution:
Torque $=$ NABI $\sin \theta$
Max Torque $=$ NABI for $\theta=90^{\circ}$
For a coil of given length $L$
Radius $=\mathrm{L} / 2 \pi \mathrm{N}$
Area $=\pi($ radius $)=\pi \times \frac{\mathrm{L}^2}{4 \pi^2 \mathrm{~N}^2}$
Torque $=\mathrm{NBI} \frac{\pi \mathrm{L}^2}{4 \pi^2 \mathrm{~N}^2}$
Torque $\alpha \frac{\mathrm{l}}{\mathrm{N}}$
$\therefore$ Smallest value of N for $\max$ Torque is $\mathrm{n}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small current carrying loop having current $i$ is placed in the plane of paper as shown. Another semicircular loop having current $i_0$ is placed concentrically in the same plane as that of small loop, the radius of semicircular loop is $R(R \gg \gt a)$. Find the force applied by the smaller ring on bigger ring in newton. (Given $R=1 \mathrm{~m}, i=i_0=\frac{40}{\sqrt{\mu_0}} A, a=0.1 \mathrm{~m}$ )
questions.solution: Magnetic field due to small ring at distance $R$ from the centre $B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{M}{R^3}$ where $M=i \pi a^2$ $\therefore \quad B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{i \pi a^2}{R^3}=\frac{\mu_0 i a^2}{4 R^3}$ $d F=i_0 R d \theta \frac{\mu_0 i^2}{4 R^3}$ $\mathrm{dF}_{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{dF} \sin \theta$ $=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{i}_0 \mathrm{i}^2 \sin \theta \mathrm{d} \theta}{4 \mathrm{R}^2}$ $F_x=\frac{\mu_0 i_0 \mathrm{i}^2}{4 R^2} \int_0^\pi \sin \theta d \theta$ $F_x=\frac{\mu_0 i i_0 a^2}{4 R^2} \times 2$ $F_x=\frac{\mu_0 i i_0 a^2}{2 R^2}$ $F_y=0$ $\therefore \quad F_{\text {net }}=F_x=\frac{\mu_0 i_0 \mathrm{i}_0 a^2}{2 R^2}$ = 8 Newton.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
